Details: Oxidation of microcystins in water by ozone is moderately to very effective (up to 100 percent removal of M-LA and M-LR in several studies) where the effectiveness is highly dependent on temperature, ozone dose, contact time, and water quality. pHs were generally below 8. In one reference, the inlet and outlet concentrations for M-LA and M-LR were given and had similar control (ranging from 50 to 100 percent). It is necessary for residual ozone to be present to oxidize the natural organic matter in the water to maximize the oxidation of microcystins.
